agent_task.title: Patch 29: Phase Refraction Layer
agent_task.purpose: Model the distortion between true/teacher phase and observed/student feature phase so continuation happens in a stable coordinate frame.
agent_task.core_hypothesis: The system is not merely failing to continue; it is continuing inside a refracted coordinate system. If the medium is stable, the refraction can be estimated and compensated.

current_state.patch_27: recursive motion anchor is leakage-free and non-frozen but still drifting
current_state.diagnosis: feature-to-phase geometry is unstable or refracted

conceptual_mapping.medium: feature mapping + admissibility + smoothing + channel/band mixing
conceptual_mapping.refraction: systematic phase offset, warping, or dispersion introduced by the medium
conceptual_mapping.constant_medium: refraction map remains stable enough to estimate
conceptual_mapping.drifting_medium: refraction map changes over time and must be tracked adaptively

non_negotiables: Do not reintroduce teacher leakage during disconnect
Do not use teacher state in student recursive update
Do not tune thresholds to force a true-continuation claim
Report whether the medium appears constant or drifting

phase_warp.definition: delta_phi(theta) learned as a circular lookup or low-order Fourier model

band_specific_refraction.definition: separate refraction parameters per rhythm band
band_specific_refraction.purpose: Prevent alpha/theta/beta collapse into one geometry.

training_phase_algorithm: For each channel and band, compute delta_phi = wrap(student_observed_theta - teacher_theta).
Compute circular mean and circular variance of delta_phi.
Estimate velocity scale eta when teacher_omega is above omega_floor.
Fit a simple refraction map: theta_observed ≈ theta_true + delta_phi(theta_true).
Store confidence = 1 - circular_variance, weighted by Signal X.
Classify medium as constant if refraction variance remains below threshold.
disconnect_phase_algorithm: Let recursive anchor produce next student observed phase.
Apply learned inverse refraction map to estimate unrefracted internal phase.
Continue dynamics in the stabilized phase frame.
Project back through refraction map only for comparison/output.
Decay refraction confidence over disconnect duration.
recouple_phase_algorithm: Measure whether learned refraction map still matches.
Update refraction model only after recouple, never during disconnect.

minimum: Refraction map has confidence >= 0.60 during train
Unrefracted-frame PLV improves over observed-frame PLV
S1_early PLV improves over Patch 27 baseline
strong: medium classified as constant or weakly_drifting
trained beats untrained in unrefracted-frame alignment

failure_modes.no_constant_refraction: delta_phi variance is too high; medium is not stable enough for simple correction.
failure_modes.wrong_inverse: correction reduces PLV or increases frequency error.
failure_modes.band_collapse: one refraction map is wrongly applied to all bands.
failure_modes.hidden_leakage: perfect correction occurs despite failed controls.
failure_modes.overfit_refraction: training improves but withheld rhythms fail.

must_include: before/after PLV in observed and unrefracted frames
S1 and S3 comparison against Patch 27

agent_task.final_instruction: Treat this patch as representation calibration, not model success. The goal is to determine whether a stable refraction map exists and whether correcting for it improves recursive continuation.
